expected_trades_per_day_basis: Top entries this exit pairs with carry trig≈3000-5000 over 30d data window (e.g., E126 trig=3443, E066 trig=3684, E036/E038/E039/E040/E041 trig=5000); using a representative trig≈3500 ÷ 30d = ~117/day raw, but liquidity_bucket=high filter typically cuts ~70% of small-cap symbols → ~35/day net.

best_in: BTC trending sessions with realized vol >3% daily on majors
sustained 30-60min directional moves on high-liquidity pairs (BTC/ETH/SOL/BNB)
funding-divergent regimes where pump_taker_buy entries get follow-through
worst_in: chop / mean-reverting ranges where 2% trail repeatedly gets clipped on retracement
low-vol weekends (BTC daily range <1.5%) — trail never activates meaningfully, degenerates to time/SL exit
sharp V-reversal regimes (e.g. macro headline whipsaws) where 2% give-back from peak swallows the entire run
regime_sensitivity.explanation: A 2% trailing step is wide enough to survive normal noise on liquid majors but is geometrically vulnerable in chop: every 2% retrace stops out, then re-entry is impossible. The high-liquidity filter narrows the universe to symbols where 2% reflects real structure rather than book-thin spikes, so edge concentrates in trending regimes on majors.

live_readiness_caveats: Trail family in R3 grid showed max≈-0.006 across 3111 combos (all negative-mean) — the synthesizer hypothesis that this is parameter mis-tune is UNVERIFIED until the {1.5, 2.0, 2.5}% sweep + activation_threshold map confirms a positive-mean island. Do NOT promote to paper-shadow on a single 2.0% point estimate.

2% trail on high-liquidity symbols implies ~$20 give-back per $1000 notional per trade vs fixed TP — at 5-10% sizing ($50-$100) that's $1-$2 of slippage absorption headroom; live execution latency >2s on trail-trigger fills will routinely eat the entire buffer.

Concurrent positions are likely: top paired entries (E036/E038/E039/E040/E041 all trig=5000) fire on overlapping signal windows on the same channel — same-symbol stack risk needs explicit dedupe or position-cap before any live consideration.

monitoring_metrics.metric: trail_activation_rate (% of trades where trail actually moved from initial SL before exit)
monitoring_metrics.rationale: If trail rarely activates, the exit degenerates to a fixed SL/time exit and the 'wide trail' hypothesis is falsified — the edge claim is then about the entry, not this exit.

monitoring_metrics.metric: median give-back from peak unrealized PnL to exit fill
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Directly measures whether the 2.0% step is being honored in live execution; persistent overshoot means the trail is too tight relative to fill latency on the liquidity_bucket=high venues.

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: deviation >40% in either direction over 5 rolling days
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Under-firing means the high-liquidity filter is stricter live than in backtest (symbol universe drift); over-firing means duplicate fills from concurrent entry signals are not being deduped.

monitoring_metrics.metric: 7-day rolling mean trade % (not p25 — R2 lesson: p25 was blind to left tail)
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Mean-based per R2 morning_brief lesson where E126 had p25=+0.395 but compounded -13.5% on paper; trail family is even more asymmetric so mean is the correct compounding proxy.
Trail exits cluster losses in chop regimes (every retrace = loss); a streak >8 signals regime mismatch before drawdown shows in P&L.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 7-day rolling mean trade % < 0 AND trail_activation_rate < 40% simultaneously
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Joint condition isolates the failure mode this archetype is most exposed to: trail not activating + losses accumulating = the wide-trail thesis is falsified, not just a vol regime dip.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Single-trade drawdown >4% on any one position (i.e. 2x the trail step without trail activating)
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Indicates trail logic or fill path is broken — the design ceiling per trade is ~2% give-back; a 4% loss means the stop didn't trigger as designed and execution risk is unbounded.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 3 consecutive days where realized BTC daily range <1.5% AND archetype mean trade % <0
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Confirms the 'worst-in low-vol' sensitivity is materializing; rather than ride out chop, pause until vol regime returns since trail family is structurally negative in low-vol per R3 grid.

expected_max_dd_basis: R3 grid trail family max≈-0.006 (effectively zero edge) on similar entries; combined with R2 E126 paper precedent of -13.5% compounding on a higher-scoring archetype, a wider trail on the same entry pool with unverified parameter tuning should be modeled at ~10-12% paper DD over a 30d window at 5-10% sizing.
compare_to_existing: Closest analog is E126 pc_pump_taker_buy_extreme_long (R2 paper-tested winner-in-backtest, -13.5% in paper) since X103 layers a trail exit onto exactly that entry family — expect similar entry-side regime dependence but with the loss profile shifted from asymmetric fixed-TP-trap toward symmetric chop-bleed; triangulate the trail_activation_rate against any existing fixed-exit baseline on E126 to isolate the exit's marginal contribution.
